variant_cdna: not_reported (human c. not given; A>G, exon 17)
genomic_coord_build: not_reported (build not stated in paper; FST x-axis 'Position kb ~28,228' is a relative window, not a genomic coordinate)
genotype_or_zygosity: 3 genotype classes AA / AG / GG; N per class AA=77, AG=174, GG=108
phenotype: Facultative skin darkness, L* (hand back, sun-exposed); higher L* = lighter. GG lightest, AA darkest (monotonic)
effect_or_association: Additive genetic model P = 5.12x10-4 (significant after Bonferroni); linear regression, covariates age+sex, PLINK v1.07
population: Northern Han Chinese (CHN), light-skinned; N=359 (210F/149M), 8 northern provinces
source_location: Fig 3A left panel + text p.1179-1180; Methods p.1183 (Genetic Association Analysis)
notes: Genotyping SnapShot/ABI3130. Mean L* AA~54.8<AG~56<GG~56.5 (read from Fig 3A).

phenotype: Constitutive skin darkness, L* (underarm, unexposed); GG lightest
Constitutive skin darkness, L* (buttock, unexposed); GG lightest
effect_or_association: Additive model P = 1.75x10-8 (strongest; significant after Bonferroni)

genotype_or_zygosity: 11 human foreskin melanocyte cell lines: 4 AA (His615 homozygous), 3 AG (het), 4 GG (Arg615 homozygous)
phenotype: Relative melanin abundance (Melanin/Protein, ug/mg) in cultured melanocytes; GG lowest, AA highest
effect_or_association: Two-tailed t-test: AA vs GG P=0.015; AG vs GG P=0.016. Means ~ AA 51, AG 36, GG 21 ug/mg (Fig 3B)
population: Human (foreskin donors, males 8-20 yr; genotype ancestry not stated, presumed CHN)
source_location: Fig 3B + text p.1180 ('Human Skins of Different rs1800414 Genotypes'); Methods p.1183
notes: Functional in-vitro confirmation of additive direction; derived-G homozygotes = least melanin.

genotype_or_zygosity: Derived G-allele population frequencies (not individual genotypes)
phenotype: Population skin pigmentation context (derived allele tracks lighter skin in East Asians)
effect_or_association: G allele: Han Chinese >50%; American Indians high; A-A China 16.0%; A-A Cambodia 21.3%; Africans 0% (absent); western Europeans <0.3% (absent/extremely rare)
population: World populations (this study: 1 CHN + 2 A-A [China, Cambodia]; others from HGDP database)
source_location: Fig 2B + text p.1179; HGDP database (hgdp.uchicago.edu, accessed 2015-07-10)
notes: East-Asian-specific variant; absent in Africans/Europeans. Basis of D2 convergent-evolution claim.

genotype_or_zygosity: not_reported (one of 8 tested OCA2-region SNPs; genotype classes in suppl. table S4, not extracted)
effect_or_association: Marginal P=0.0395 (buttock only); becomes nonsignificant P=0.632 when conditioned on rs1800414
source_location: Text p.1180; supplementary table S4 (not obtained)
notes: Signal attributable to LD with rs1800414 (conditional analysis). Not an independent effect.

rsid: not_reported (6 further SNPs of the 8 tested; IDs in suppl. table S4)
phenotype: Skin darkness L* (hand back / underarm / buttock)
source_location: Text p.1180; supplementary table S4 (not obtained)
notes: 8 SNPs total analyzed (5 inside 44.2kb selection region + 3 outside). rsIDs re-openable from suppl. table S4.

rsid: rs1426654 (rsID not printed in this paper; canonical for Ala111Thr, cited from Lamason 2005)
genotype_or_zygosity: Population allele frequency: nearly fixed (>95%) in Europeans; extremely rare in Africans and East Asians
phenotype: Light skin in Europeans (the canonical European causal variant)
effect_or_association: Nearly fixed in Europeans due to local positive selection; 'the most striking' European light-skin allele
population: Europeans (>95%); rare in Africans and East Asians
source_location: Text p.1178 (Intro) + Discussion p.1182; cited to Lamason et al. 2005
notes: CONTEXT/literature-cited, not genotyped in this study. Central to D2: East Asians are light-skinned but LACK this usual causal variant.

variant_cdna: A>G at +1829 (A4G nucleotide substitution, zebrafish oca2 CDS)
genotype_or_zygosity: mRNA rescue: wt(A)/His610 vs mt(G)/His610Arg, 100 pg co-injected into oca2-morphants (oca2-MO knockdown)
phenotype: Melanophore pigmentation rescue at 36 hpf (Class I-IV scoring, N=137/111/107/96)
effect_or_association: wt(A) mRNA restored pigmentation; mt(G)/His610Arg mRNA FAILED to restore -> derived allele is loss-of-function for melanin
notes: Cross-species functional validation of causal direction of the His->Arg substitution.

genotype_or_zygosity: TM strain homozygous for derived-allele-like His610Arg; plus KO (frameshift, truncated Oca2), WT (C57BL/6 black), WT' (2 synonymous control mutations)
phenotype: Coat/skin/paw pigmentation (L*); TM = light (brown/gray hair, light tail skin/paws, iris unchanged); KO = albino (white hair/skin/paws, pink iris); WT/WT' = black
effect_or_association: TM significantly lighter than WT/WT' (Wilcoxon rank sum, P<0.05); reduced mature melanosomes (TEM). N: 4 WT, 4 WT', 6 TM, 6 KO
source_location: Fig 4C,D,E + text p.1180-1182; Methods p.1184-1185; suppl. fig S8/S9, tables S6/S7
notes: In-vivo validation: homozygous His610Arg -> light coloration, distinct from albino KO. Synonymous WT' control rules out CRISPR artifact.
